value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $1.927B / mcap $26.36B = 7.31% yield, comfortably above 7% threshold.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $83 target / $6.13 fwd EPS = 13.5x vs fwd PE 8.99x = 33.6% discount.

drawdown.detail: Down 36.67% from 52w high $87.03; price near 52w low $54.26.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $740M (cash $1.914B - debt $1.174B); CR 2.14, well above 1.2.

dividend.detail: Yield 2.39% with payout 27.2% — very sustainable, ample room for growth.

institutional.detail: Inst held 105.8% (overlapping holdings); rec_mean 2.14 with 13/28 buys.

ai_exposure.detail: IT services revenue is exposed to GenAI-driven productivity gains compressing billable hours; mitigated only if Cognizant successfully repositions as an AI implementation partner — currently a structural overhang for the sector.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest rose 14.6% MoM to 9.83% (6.56 days-to-cover) — meaningful bear pressure but not extreme; a re-rating could trigger covering.

catalysts_general: Trading at 33.6% discount to analyst median $83 target
FCF yield 7.3% with debt-free balance sheet
Forward PE 8.99x with 22.1% earnings growth and PEG 0.9
Dividend payout only 27% leaves room for buybacks/hikes

downside_scenarios.scenario: Sector multiple compression on GenAI service-revenue concerns
IT services bear case: bookings deceleration + margin pressure
Recession + structural AI displacement of staff augmentation

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below 52w low $54.26 to confirm capitulation; deep value with 7.5%+ FCF yield and 2.5%+ dividend at this level.

comparables.note: Direct peer; CTSH trades at meaningful discount despite similar margins
Higher growth but higher beta and short interest
Not in input data; broader IT services context — CTSH cheap on every metric

value_picks.analyst_take: CTSH is a textbook deep-value setup with all six framework criteria passing: 7.3% FCF yield, 33% discount to analyst targets, near 52w low, and the only dividend-paying name in the cohort. The risk is structural (AI displacing services labor) rather than financial — balance sheet is pristine. BUY with limit near $54.

value_picks.name: Accenture plc

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $12.16B / mcap $109.4B = 11.12% — exceptional for a mega-cap.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $250 target / $14.91 fwd EPS = 16.8x vs fwd PE 11.92x = 28.9% discount, just below the 30% pass bar.

dividend.detail: Yield 3.67% at payout 51% — strong and sustainable.

institutional.detail: Inst held 82.8%; rec_mean 1.86 with 18/28 buys including 4 strong buys.

ai_exposure.detail: Accenture is the world's largest GenAI consulting partner (>$3B GenAI bookings disclosed); the firm is the implementation channel of choice for Microsoft/Google/Salesforce AI rollouts. Near-term services revenue may compress, but AI is a clear long-term tailwind given competitive moat.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest rose 22.5% MoM to 3.27% — building skepticism but absolute level still low; well-covered by 0.5x ADV.

catalysts_general: Trading at 28.9% discount to analyst median $250 target
FCF yield 11% — top-decile for a $100B+ tech company
3.67% dividend yield with 51% payout, room for hikes
ROE 24.8% with operating margins 13.8%

downside_scenarios.scenario: Bookings slowdown extends into 2H; services demand soft
Recession-driven enterprise IT freeze; multiple compresses to GFC level
Severe structural AI displacement thesis confirmed

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52w low $173.84 — confirmation buy. FCF yield exceeds 11% and dividend yield approaches 3.7% at this level.

comparables.note: Cheaper sibling with similar dividend; ACN commands premium for scale + GenAI lead
Faster growth but smaller scale and Eastern-Europe risk
Not in input data; ACN trades at half IBM's multiple with much better growth

value_picks.analyst_take: ACN is a high-quality compounder selling at recession-bucket multiples after a 45% drawdown. The franchise has the strongest GenAI implementation partnerships in services and an 11% FCF yield with a 3.7% dividend. Buy near 52w low; risk is sector-wide services-billing compression, not company-specific.

value_picks.name: Atlassian Corporation

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $1.524B / mcap $18.39B = 8.29% — exceptional for a 23% grower.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $130 target / $5.55 fwd EPS = 23.4x vs fwd PE 12.55x = 46.4% discount.

drawdown.detail: Down 71.19% from $242 — second-largest drawdown in cohort.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $351M (cash $1.566B - debt $1.215B); framework allows pass via net-cash branch despite CR 0.89.

dividend.detail: No dividend — neutral, counted as borderline (0.5).

institutional.detail: Inst held 95.1%; rec_mean 1.58 — strongest of the software cohort with 26/33 buys.

ai_exposure.detail: Atlassian Intelligence (Rovo agents) embedded across Jira/Confluence creates pricing power on top of installed base; AI-driven seat-expansion is the bull thesis. Threat from copilot-style productivity tools is real but offset by workflow lock-in.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest spiked 28% MoM to 7.92% — capitulation positioning into a stock already 71% off highs; squeeze risk is asymmetric.

catalysts_general: Trading at 46.4% discount to analyst median $130 target
FCF yield 8.3% on a 23% revenue grower (rare combination)
Strongest analyst conviction in cohort (rec_mean 1.58, 5 strong buys)
PEG 0.64 — extremely cheap on growth-adjusted basis

downside_scenarios.scenario: Recession + SaaS net retention compression to <100%
GAAP losses persist; market rotates from unprofitable software
Severe SMB cyclicality + AI-driven seat erosion

ideal_entry.reasoning: Above 52w low $56.01 with first-half-of-range positioning; FCF yield exceeds 9.5% at this level and analyst median target implies 117% upside.

comparables.note: Larger ARR base, slower growth; both cheap on fwd PE
Comparable growth but TEAM trades at 30% discount
Not in input data; smaller competitor in work-mgmt at richer multiple — TEAM relatively cheap

value_picks.analyst_take: TEAM is an asymmetric setup: a 23% revenue grower with 8% FCF yield trading at 12.5x fwd EPS after a 71% drawdown. GAAP losses keep value buyers away while growth buyers fled the multiple compression — that gap is the opportunity. Buy near $60-65 with awareness that operating margins remain negative.

value_picks.name: Workday, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $3.013B / mcap $31.15B = 9.67% — outstanding for an enterprise SaaS leader.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $177.5 target / $12.44 fwd EPS = 14.3x vs fwd PE 9.74x = 31.7% discount.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.62B (cash $5.44B - debt $3.82B); D/E 49 (just under 50), CR 1.32.

dividend.detail: No dividend — neutral, counted as borderline (0.5).

ai_exposure.detail: Workday's HR data is the source-of-truth layer for AI agents in workforce planning, recruiting, and FP&A; AI accelerates seat expansion via 'Workday Illuminate' GenAI features rather than displacing the platform.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest jumped 23.9% MoM to 9.23% — bearish positioning at multi-year price low; high beta-of-bounce setup.

catalysts_general: Trading at 31.7% discount to analyst median $177.5 target
FCF yield 9.7% — highest among software peers in cohort
PEG 0.46 (cheapest growth-adjusted in cohort)
62.9% earnings growth with 14.5% revenue growth

downside_scenarios.scenario: HCM cycle slowdown + AI seat compression
Structural displacement by horizontal AI HR agents

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52w low $110.36; FCF yield exceeds 10% and 47% upside to analyst median target at this level.

comparables.note: Premium peer at nearly 2x WDAY's multiple — relative value clear
Higher growth but smaller scale and GAAP losses
Not in input data; another enterprise SaaS peer at higher multiple

value_picks.analyst_take: WDAY is the cohort's value/growth standout: 14.5% revenue growth, 9.7% FCF yield, and 9.7x fwd PE — all at a 56% drawdown from highs. PEG 0.46 is jarringly cheap for an HR data monopoly. Buy near 52w low; primary risk is AI-agent disruption of HR workflows but Workday is more likely the AI beneficiary than the victim.

value_picks.name: EPAM Systems, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $193 target / $13.92 fwd EPS = 13.9x vs fwd PE 8.20x = 40.9% discount.

dividend.detail: No dividend — neutral, counted as borderline (0.5).

ai_exposure.detail: EPAM's core business is staff-augmentation engineering — the most directly exposed segment of IT services to coding-copilot productivity gains. Lower realized billable hours per project is a structural risk; offset partially by AI-implementation work but on net negative.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 18.35% (5.5 days-to-cover) — extreme bearish positioning; small float ($6B mcap) creates squeeze potential but also confirms market skepticism.

catalysts_general: Trading at 40.9% discount to analyst median $193 target
FCF yield 12% with $1.15B net cash on $6B mcap
PEG 0.71 with 12.8% revenue growth
Highest short interest in cohort (18.35%) — squeeze setup

downside_scenarios.scenario: Severe AI displacement of staff augmentation services

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just below 52w low $113.83 to confirm capitulation; framework score is strong but small-cap + 18% short + AI-services threat warrant a confirmation buy rather than chasing.

comparables.note: Larger scale, less risk premium, less upside
Not in input data; Indian IT services context — EPAM dramatically cheaper but with more risk

value_picks.analyst_take: EPAM checks every quantitative box (12% FCF yield, 41% PE discount, net cash) but combines the cohort's highest AI-displacement exposure with 18% short interest and small-cap volatility. Framework supports BUY but qualitative risk profile warrants WATCHLIST until the stock confirms support below $114 or a fundamental beat re-rates the multiple.

value_picks.name: ServiceNow, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.108B / mcap $93.32B = 5.47% — in the 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Implied PE from $140 target / $5.01 fwd EPS = 28.0x vs fwd PE 18.07x = 35.4% discount.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $2.75B (cash $5.18B - debt $2.43B); framework passes via net-cash branch despite CR 0.85.

dividend.detail: No dividend — neutral, counted as borderline (0.5).

institutional.detail: Inst held 91.0%; rec_mean 1.44 — strongest analyst rating in cohort with 43/48 buys.

ai_exposure.detail: ServiceNow's Now Assist GenAI agents have been the fastest-monetizing enterprise AI product to date; the workflow platform is the orchestration layer for AI agents across enterprise functions. Strongest AI tailwind in cohort.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest spiked 39.6% MoM to 3.78% — sharpest increase in cohort, but absolute level still low and well-covered; suggests tactical skepticism at recent capitulation.

catalysts_general: Trading at 35.4% discount to analyst median $140 target
Strongest analyst conviction in cohort (rec_mean 1.44, 9 strong buys)
22.1% revenue growth — fastest top-line in cohort
Cleanest AI monetization story among large-cap software

downside_scenarios.scenario: Premium-software multiple compression on macro slowdown
Severe recession + AI commoditization of workflow platforms

ideal_entry.reasoning: Above 52w low $81.24; FCF yield improves to ~6% and the analyst-target upside expands to 67% at this level.

comparables.note: Cheaper but slower-growing enterprise SaaS peer
Similar growth at lower multiple but smaller scale and GAAP losses
Not in input data; comparable workflow/CRM platform peer

value_picks.analyst_take: NOW carries the cohort's premium multiple (18x fwd) but delivers the cohort's best growth (22% revenue) and clearest AI monetization. After a 57% drawdown the fwd PE is the lowest in years for the franchise. FCF yield is only borderline at 5.5%, which keeps this from being a pure value play, but quality+growth+AI-leverage justify BUY at these levels.
